execSummary: Jahez International Company (Tadawul: 6017) is a Saudi-listed quick-commerce and food-delivery group; the Q4-2025 consolidation of Snoonu extends the group beyond single-vertical food delivery into broader last-mile logistics.

FY2025A GMV of {fy25.gmv} grew +{calc.gmvGrowthFy25} YoY on {fy25.orders} orders (+{calc.ordersGrowthFy25} YoY) at an average order value of {fy25.aov}; net revenue reached {fy25.net_revenue} (+{calc.netRevGrowthFy25} YoY) at a {fy25.take_rate} take rate ({fy24.take_rate} FY2024A).

Net income compressed {calc.netIncomeDeclineFy25Magnitude} YoY to {fy25.net_income}, carrying {fy25.one_off} of Q4-2025 one-off items (Red Color impairment, Marn goodwill impairment, Others-segment ECL, Snoonu deal costs); Q1-2026A printed a {calc.q1LossMagnitude} net loss while adjusted EBITDA remained positive at {q1_26.adj_ebitda}.

Adjusted EBITDA margin contracted ~{calc.ebitdaMarginContractionBps} bps to {fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} (FY2024A: {fy24.adj_ebitda_margin}), and gross margin moved to {fy25.gross_margin} from {fy24.gross_margin}, consistent with a market absorbing delivery-fee competition.

Snoonu contributed {fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} of Group GMV ({fy25.snoonu_gmv}) from an October consolidation start and {fy25.snoonu_adj_ebitda} of positive adjusted EBITDA; the Logistics segment posted {fy25.segment_logistics_net_revenue} of FY2025A net revenue.

Faheem's DCF (WACC {calc.wacc}, terminal growth {calc.terminalGrowth}) indicates a base-case value of {calc.targetPrice} per share against the {calc.currentPrice} close, {calc.upside} implied upside; the trading-comps cross-check spans {calc.compsMin} to {calc.compsMax} (median {calc.compsMedian}).

Scenario analysis weights bull, base and bear cases at 25% / 50% / 25%, giving a probability-weighted value of {calc.weightedPerShare} per share and a weighted expected return of {calc.weightedReturn} against Lunar's {calc.hurdle} Benchmark; the bear case in isolation returns {calc.irrBear}, below the Benchmark.

The balance sheet carries {calc.netCash} of net cash, and the compliance screen passes both IC Charter ratio tests ({calc.debtRatio} debt / market cap and {calc.cashRatio} cash / market cap, against 33% thresholds).
The quantified risk register rolls up to a composite score of {calc.riskScore} / 10, with price-war margin compression scored as the dominant risk.
This memorandum presents the analysis without an investment recommendation: the investment decision rests with the Investment Committee.

returnAnalysis.approach: Faheem applied two valuation approaches to the position: a discounted cash flow on free cash flow to the firm as the primary methodology, and a trading-comparables cross-check. The DCF builds the discount rate bottom-up from a {calc.riskFreeRate} Saudi risk-free rate, a {calc.beta} comp-set beta and a {calc.equityRiskPremium} equity risk premium, giving a {calc.costOfEquity} cost of equity and a {calc.wacc} WACC. Discounting the explicit FY2026E-FY2030E forecast plus a Gordon terminal value at {calc.terminalGrowth} terminal growth, and adding {calc.netCash} of net cash, indicates {calc.targetPrice} per share, {calc.upside} against the {calc.currentPrice} close.
returnAnalysis.compsNote: The comparables cross-check applies EV/Revenue, EV/EBITDA and P/E multiples from Talabat, DoorDash and Delivery Hero to Jahez's FY2025A figures. The implied-value field is wide, {calc.compsMin} to {calc.compsMax} with a median of {calc.compsMedian}, reflecting the gap between regional-scale multiples and richly priced US growth comps. The DCF result sits toward the lower half of that field and is anchored to Jahez's own projected cash flows rather than borrowed multiples; it is the methodology this memorandum relies on.
returnAnalysis.scenarios: Bull, base and bear cases apply parallel shifts to net-revenue growth and EBITDA margin at probability weights of 25% / 50% / 25%, an analyst judgment documented in the workbook's Assumptions tab. The probability-weighted value per share is {calc.weightedPerShare} and the weighted expected return over the 4-year hold is {calc.weightedReturn}. The bear case ({calc.perShareBear} per share, {calc.irrBear} IRR) falls below the {calc.hurdle} Benchmark in isolation; the weighted case clears it.
returnAnalysis.benchmarkCheck: Against the Lunar Investment Mandate: the scenario-weighted expected return of {calc.weightedReturn} clears the {calc.hurdle} gross-IRR Benchmark (Lunar IC Charter, p.3), with the base case at {calc.irrBase}. A position would be sized within the mandate's 10% single-name concentration cap (Lunar IC Charter, p.4), subject to ticket sizing at the Investment Committee's discretion, and the Tadawul listing (6017) with active daily trading satisfies the liquidity requirement for public-equity allocations (Lunar IC Charter, p.4). Because the bear case in isolation ({calc.irrBear}) sits below the Benchmark, the mandate-fit case rests on the probability-weighted return, and the Committee should weigh that sensitivity explicitly.

strengths.title: Diversification beyond single-vertical delivery
strengths.body: The Q4-2025 Snoonu consolidation contributed {fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} of Group GMV ({fy25.snoonu_gmv}) from an October start and {fy25.snoonu_adj_ebitda} of positive adjusted EBITDA, and the Logistics segment reached {fy25.segment_logistics_net_revenue} of FY2025A net revenue. The revenue base is measurably less dependent on the single KSA food-delivery vertical than a year earlier.

strengths.title: Volume growth sustained through a competitive market
strengths.body: Orders grew +{calc.ordersGrowthFy25} YoY to {fy25.orders} and GMV +{calc.gmvGrowthFy25} to {fy25.gmv} in FY2025A, while group take rate held at {fy25.take_rate} ({fy24.take_rate} FY2024A): commission monetisation was maintained through a period of delivery-fee discounting across the market.

strengths.title: Positive operating cash profit through a loss-making quarter
strengths.body: FY2025A adjusted EBITDA of {fy25.adj_ebitda} and Q1-2026A adjusted EBITDA of {q1_26.adj_ebitda} remained positive through the {calc.q1LossMagnitude} Q1-2026A net loss; the identified Q4-2025 one-off items ({fy25.one_off}) are itemised in the filings and sit outside the adjusted-EBITDA line.

strengths.title: Net-cash, asset-light balance sheet
strengths.body: The balance sheet carries {calc.netCash} of net cash against {q1_26.islamic_facilities_loans} of Islamic facilities and loans (Q1 2026 Financial Statements). The platform model requires minimal fixed capital, and both IC Charter compliance ratios sit inside the 33% threshold ({calc.debtRatio} debt / market cap, {calc.cashRatio} cash / market cap).

concerns.title: Price-war margin compression
concerns.body: Delivery-fee discounting led by the well-funded Keeta entry is the dominant modelled risk: adjusted EBITDA margin contracted ~{calc.ebitdaMarginContractionBps} bps to {fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} in FY2025A and Q1-2026A printed a {calc.q1LossMagnitude} net loss. The bear case prices a deeper and longer compression ({calc.perShareBear} per share, {calc.irrBear} IRR).

concerns.title: Further well-capitalised entrants
concerns.body: Beyond the incumbent field, Rabbit, the Ninja unicorn and Dingdong are identified in the industry pack as funded entrants competing primarily on price (Industry & News Pack, p.2, p.4). Sustained entry would extend the discounting cycle the FY2025A margins already reflect.

concerns.title: One-off items and impairment recurrence
concerns.body: FY2025A carried {fy25.one_off} of Q4 one-off items across the Red Color impairment, Marn goodwill impairment, Others-segment ECL and Snoonu deal costs. A further impairment cycle would indicate the items are not fully one-time; the monitoring plan treats any recurrence as a trigger to re-run the scenario weights.

concerns.title: Bear case falls below the Benchmark
concerns.body: The mandate-fit case rests on the {calc.weightedReturn} probability-weighted return rather than any single scenario: the bear case in isolation returns {calc.irrBear}, below the {calc.hurdle} Benchmark. The 25% / 50% / 25% weighting is an analyst judgment and is documented as such.

concerns.title: Forecast dependence on margin recovery
concerns.body: The valuation assumes adjusted EBITDA margin recovers from {fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} toward the FY2024A level ({fy24.adj_ebitda_margin}) by the terminal year, anchored to management's FY2026 guidance and Snoonu's full consolidated year. The recovery path is an assumption chain documented in the workbook, not a disclosed company plan.

memo.riskIntro: The quantified risk register below scores each identified risk on a 1-5 probability x 1-5 impact basis, rolling up to a composite, peak-weighted score of {calc.riskScore} out of 10. The highest-scored risk is price-war margin compression from well-funded discounting, the dynamic visible in the ~{calc.ebitdaMarginContractionBps} bps FY2025A EBITDA-margin contraction and the {calc.q1LossMagnitude} Q1-2026A net loss, followed by the arrival of further well-capitalised entrants. Both are sector-wide pressures rather than company-specific execution failures, and both are reflected in the bear-case scenario ({calc.irrBear} IRR, {calc.perShareBear} per share) rather than assumed away.
memo.companyOverview: Jahez operates a quick-commerce and food-delivery platform in Saudi Arabia, listed on the Tadawul Main Market (6017). The FY2025A revenue base spans the core KSA delivery platform ({fy25.segment_ksa_net_revenue} of net revenue, {fy25.segment_ksa_adj_ebitda} of adjusted EBITDA), a Logistics segment ({fy25.segment_logistics_net_revenue}), and Non-KSA and Others segments that remain sub-scale or loss-making. The Q4-2025 acquisition of Snoonu extends the group beyond single-vertical food delivery into broader last-mile logistics: Snoonu contributed {fy25.snoonu_gmv} of GMV ({fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} of Group GMV from an October consolidation start) with {fy25.snoonu_gross_revenue} of gross revenue and {fy25.snoonu_adj_ebitda} of adjusted EBITDA.
memo.marketOverview: The Saudi quick-commerce and food-delivery market is contested by HungerStation, the well-funded entrant Keeta, and a wave of further capitalised players, Rabbit, the Ninja unicorn, and Dingdong, competing primarily on delivery-fee discounting rather than product differentiation (Industry & News Pack · Saudi Quick-Commerce, p.2). Group take rate rose to {fy25.take_rate} in FY2025A from {fy24.take_rate} in FY2024A even as GOV grew to {fy25.gov}: commission monetisation held through the discounting cycle, though the price war (Industry & News Pack, p.4) remains the dominant near-term swing factor for sector margins. This memorandum does not assert a specific market-share position for any player absent a disclosed source.

financials.historical: Orders grew from {fy23.orders} (FY2023A) to {fy24.orders} (FY2024A) to {fy25.orders} (FY2025A), against average order values of {fy23.aov}, {fy24.aov} and {fy25.aov}; GMV compounded from {fy23.gmv} to {fy24.gmv} to {fy25.gmv}. Take rate rose from {fy23.take_rate} to {fy24.take_rate} to {fy25.take_rate}, lifting commission revenue to {fy25.commission_revenue}, while net revenue growth decelerated to +{calc.netRevGrowthFy25} YoY: the gap between a rising take rate and slowing net-revenue growth is the price-war signature, a larger monetised share of a GMV base that is itself growing more slowly. Adjusted EBITDA moved from {fy23.adj_ebitda} to {fy24.adj_ebitda} to {fy25.adj_ebitda}, and depreciation and amortisation grew from {fy24.dna} to {fy25.dna}, reflecting the expanding IFRS-16 right-of-use base.
financials.forecast: The forecast rebuilds adjusted EBITDA margin from a depressed FY2026E base toward the FY2024A level by FY2030E, anchored to management's FY2026 guidance, the roll-off of the identified Q4-2025 one-offs, and Snoonu's first full consolidated year; each driver (order growth, AOV, monetisation rate, margin path, capex, working capital) is a documented assumption in the workbook's Assumptions tab with its rationale stated. Free cash flow to the firm is forecast-only: FY2023A-FY2025A capex is not separately disclosed, so the actual-year FCFF shown in the table is an estimate for continuity and does not feed the DCF. The explicit-period FCFF is discounted at a {calc.wacc} WACC with a {calc.terminalGrowth} Gordon terminal growth rate; the resulting enterprise value, net of {calc.netCash} of net cash, implies {calc.targetPrice} per share.

memo.compliance: Jahez passes Lunar's IC Charter compliance screen (Tadawul-aligned, on IFRS figures as endorsed by SOCPA) on both financial-ratio tests: interest-bearing debt, {q1_26.islamic_facilities_loans} of Islamic facilities and loans (Q1 2026 Financial Statements), sits at {calc.debtRatio} of market capitalisation, and cash & interest-bearing securities, {q1_26.cash} of cash (Q1 2026 Financial Statements; interest-bearing securities are not separately disclosed, so the screen is run on cash alone), sits at {calc.cashRatio}, both comfortably inside the Charter's 33% leverage/liquidity threshold. Including IFRS-16 lease liabilities ({q1_26.lease_liabilities}) in the stricter memo view still passes at {calc.leaseInclRatio}. Non-eligible income is not separately disclosed in the filings; per the Charter's methodology the position is screened via the business-activity test instead, food delivery and logistics is an eligible business activity, financed via Islamic facilities, rather than a fabricated income percentage. Overall Compliance screen: {calc.complianceStatus}.
memo.catalysts: Near-term catalysts: Snoonu's first full financial year of consolidation (the FY2025 contribution was {fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} of Group GMV from an October start; a full FY2026 should show materially more), the path back to positive net income after the {calc.q1LossMagnitude} Q1-2026A loss, and the trajectory of take rate ({fy25.take_rate} in FY2025A) as a read on how much of the Keeta-led price war Jahez can pass through versus absorb. The Investment Committee should treat any further one-off impairment (Q4-2025A already carried {fy25.one_off} across Red Color, Marn goodwill, Others-segment ECL and Snoonu deal costs) as a trigger to re-run the scenario weights, not as one-time noise to dismiss.
monitoringKpis: GMV growth, {fy25.gmv} FY2025A (+{calc.gmvGrowthFy25} YoY); watch for deceleration below the FY2026E-FY2030E forecast path
Take rate, {fy25.take_rate} FY2025A vs {fy24.take_rate} FY2024A; the primary lever on net revenue given a competitive GMV environment
Adjusted EBITDA margin, {fy25.adj_ebitda_margin} FY2025A vs {fy24.adj_ebitda_margin} FY2024A; the recovery path is the single biggest swing factor in the valuation
Net income / net loss, {fy25.net_income} FY2025A, {q1_26.net_loss} Q1-2026A; watch for a return to consistent profitability
Cash & net cash position, {q1_26.cash} cash, {calc.netCash} net cash as of Q1-2026; supports the compliance screen and funds continued logistics build-out
Snoonu contribution, {fy25.snoonu_gmv_contribution} of FY2025A Group GMV from an October consolidation start; the full-year FY2026 read-through is the key diversification proof point
